name: personal-configs
description: Claude Code plugins for AI-assisted development workflows

plugins.name: core-workflow
plugins.description: Lean set of commands, skills, and an agent for TDD, debugging, plan review, research rigor, LaTeX reports, codebase understanding, and reviewing collaborators' remote changes

plugins.name: clear-writing
plugins.description: Write and communicate in a clear, plain style with no slop.

plugins.name: playwright
plugins.description: Browser automation with Playwright - test pages, fill forms, take screenshots, validate UX, and automate any browser task

plugins.name: infrastructure-as-code
plugins.description: Terraform and AWS infrastructure management with safety-first practices

plugins.name: notify
plugins.description: Terminal bell and macOS banner notifications when Claude Code needs input or finishes responding

plugins.name: precise-technical-communication
plugins.description: Standing rules for explaining technical work in plain, exact, auditable language - scope, method, evidence, definitions, assumptions, verification, and limitations

plugins.name: codebase-hygiene
plugins.description: Skills and a pre-commit hook for keeping a codebase organized, evergreen, and documented, with AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md pairing and an optional per-repo .documentation-check manifest

plugins.name: python-code-quality
plugins.description: Python code-quality principles: verification-first, runtime-validated (Pydantic at boundaries), legible artifacts, with anti-overengineering guardrails

plugins.name: export-to-clipboard
plugins.description: User-invoked skill to export the current Claude Code session transcript (full or last N turns) as Markdown, writing to a local Obsidian vault or copying to the clipboard via OSC 52 when run remotely

plugins.name: conceptual-thought-partner
plugins.description: A Claude Fable subagent that acts as a senior engineering and research thought partner - stress-tests approaches, reviews architecture, and reasons through ambiguous problems conversationally, without ever doing implementation work
